title: Cryptic error message when dropping a column part of a unique constraint
description:
### What happens?

When dropping a column that is a part of a unique constraint, duckdb will tell you that there is no column with that name.

A better error message is advisable.

### To Reproduce

```
D CREATE TABLE test2 (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT, surname TEXT, age INT, UNIQUE(surname, age));
D DESCRIBE test2;
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ column_name в”Ӯ column_type в”Ӯ  null   в”Ӯ   key   в”Ӯ default в”Ӯ  extra  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ   varchar   в”Ӯ   varchar   в”Ӯ varchar в”Ӯ varchar в”Ӯ varchar в”Ӯ varchar в”Ӯ
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
в”Ӯ id          в”Ӯ INTEGER     в”Ӯ NO      в”Ӯ PRI     в”Ӯ NULL    в”Ӯ NULL    в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ name        в”Ӯ VARCHAR     в”Ӯ YES     в”Ӯ NULL    в”Ӯ NULL    в”Ӯ NULL    в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ surname     в”Ӯ VARCHAR     в”Ӯ YES     в”Ӯ UNI     в”Ӯ NULL    в”Ӯ NULL    в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ age         в”Ӯ INTEGER     в”Ӯ YES     в”Ӯ UNI     в”Ӯ NULL    в”Ӯ NULL    в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
D ALTER TABLE test2 DROP COLUMN surname;
Catalog Error:
table "test2" does not have a column named "surname"
```

### OS:

Windows 11

### DuckDB Version:

v1.3.0 

### DuckDB Client:

CLI

### Hardware:

_No response_

### Full Name:

Raffaele Mancuso

### Affiliation:

University of Bologna

### What is the latest build you tested with? If possible, we recommend testing with the latest nightly build.

I have tested with a stable release

### Did you include all relevant data sets for reproducing the issue?

Yes

### Did you include all code required to reproduce the issue?

- [x] Yes, I have

- [x] Yes, I have
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test3 (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, description VARCHAR, value DECIMAL(10, 2), UNIQUE(description, value));
INSERT INTO test3 (id, description, value) VALUES (1, 'Item A', 10.50);
ALTER TABLE test3 DROP ATTRIBUTE value;
SELECT * FROM test3 WHERE description = 'Item A';
CREATE TABLE test4 (user_id INTEGER, email VARCHAR, age INTEGER, UNIQUE(email, age));
key_bug_features: Dropping column part of unique constraint triggers incorrect error message
